description: A conceptual diagram illustrating the relationship between a real ground deformation vector and its components measured along satellite Line Of Sight (LOS) for ascending and descending passes. The diagram shows a Measurement Point (MP) located on a terrain profile. The vertical axis is labelled 'h' for height, and the horizontal axis is labelled 'ground range'. Two satellite icons are depicted, indicating the origin of the `LOS Asc` (Ascending) and `LOS Desc` (Descending) directions, which are represented by dashed grey lines pointing towards the MP. A green vector, labelled `d_real`, originates from the MP and represents the true, real-world ground deformation. Two red vectors, `d_Desc` and `d_Asc`, represent the measured deformation components along the Descending and Ascending LOS directions, respectively. Dotted green lines connect the endpoints of `d_Desc` and `d_Asc` to the endpoint of `d_real`, illustrating that `d_real` is the resultant vector reconstructed from these LOS components.
